Title: A glycosyl transferase family 43 protein involved in xylan biosynthesis is associated with straw digestibility in Brachypodium distachyon

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT43 glycosyltransferase family; IRX14-like xylan biosynthesis protein
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem secondary cell wall; straw cell wall, especially 1 M KOH-extractable matrix polysaccharide fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major grass cell wall matrix polysaccharide affecting biomass recalcitrance and straw digestibility
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan/arabinoxylan biosynthesis in secondary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Xylan backbone biosynthesis module of grass secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream polymer assembly step in xylan backbone formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for synthesis of target polysaccharide backbone and thereby for arabinoxylan accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to participate in synthesis/elongation of the β-1,4-xylan backbone during xylan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; supported as a major determinant of straw digestibility-associated QTL
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in stem and stem tip/inflorescence stem libraries; also expressed in flower
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stem tissue; abundant in inflorescence stems and stem tip/base libraries
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth after vernalization; saccharification assay included mild alkaline pretreatment before cellulase digestion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; candidate gene analysis; parental allele sequencing/SNP identification; phylogenetic analysis; RNAi knockdown in Brachypodium; qRT-PCR; saccharification assay; cell wall monosaccharide profiling; ferulic acid and p-coumaric acid quantification; xylan molecular weight analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genotype SNP data from Brachypodium RIL population (from Cui et al., 2012); in silico expression libraries from Phytozome v3.1
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IRX14 orthologous xylan biosynthesis machinery (exact interactors not experimentally tested in this paper)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced BdGT43A expression increases straw saccharification and decreases stem cell wall xylose, arabinose, and ferulic acid, with increased p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A major QTL for straw digestibility on chromosome 5 colocalized with BdGT43A/Bradi5g24290.1. 2. BdGT43A is strongly expressed in stems and carries a nonsynonymous parental SNP causing an Ala80Thr substitution predicted to affect protein function. 3. RNAi knockdown of BdGT43A by about 70–75% increased saccharification in stems. 4. Silenced lines had reduced xylose and arabinose in the 1 M KOH cell wall fraction, consistent with reduced arabinoxylan. 5. Silenced lines also showed reduced ferulic acid and increased p-coumaric acid, linking altered arabinoxylan composition to improved digestibility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve straw digestibility in grasses/crops by downregulation or allele selection; useful for biofuel/feed improvement with limited visible growth penalty in the tested knockdown lines; should monitor wall composition and mechanical integrity in deployment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible phenotype reported in RNAi lines, but altered cell wall composition could potentially affect strength or other wall-related traits; exact long-term agronomic penalties not assessed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (constitutive, for gus marker in transformation vector); CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive, for hpt selectable marker). Promoter driving the amiRNA/RNAi insert not clearly stated in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers BdGT43A is the causative gene underlying the QTL, but direct biochemical enzymatic assay was not performed. No significant change in xylan chain length was detected despite reduced xylan abundance. Increased digestibility in grass differs from Arabidopsis irx14 comparison, possibly because grass arabinoxylan feruloylation is especially important.
